verses.text: Four chariots come from between two mountains of brass, with horses red, black, white, and grisled. Heaven's forces. God dispatches His agents to every quarter of the earth.
Four chariots come from between two mountains of brass, with horses red, black, white, and grisled. Heaven's forces. God dispatches His agents to every quarter of the earth.
Four chariots come from between two mountains of brass, with horses red, black, white, and grisled. Heaven's forces. God dispatches His agents to every quarter of the earth.
These are 'the four spirits of the heavens,' going forth from before the Lord of all the earth. Sent by God. The powers that move the world go out at His command.
These are 'the four spirits of the heavens,' going forth from before the Lord of all the earth. Sent by God. The powers that move the world go out at His command.
These are 'the four spirits of the heavens,' going forth from before the Lord of all the earth. Sent by God. The powers that move the world go out at His command.
They walk to and fro through the earth; those toward the north 'have quieted my spirit' there. Judgment executed. God's purpose against the oppressor is carried out.
They walk to and fro through the earth; those toward the north 'have quieted my spirit' there. Judgment executed. God's purpose against the oppressor is carried out.
Take silver and gold of the captives, make crowns, and set them on Joshua the high priest's head. A symbolic crowning. The priest is crowned to foreshadow a greater One.
Take silver and gold of the captives, make crowns, and set them on Joshua the high priest's head. A symbolic crowning. The priest is crowned to foreshadow a greater One.
Take silver and gold of the captives, make crowns, and set them on Joshua the high priest's head. A symbolic crowning. The priest is crowned to foreshadow a greater One.

'Behold the man whose name is The BRANCH... he shall build the temple of the LORD,' bearing the glory, 'a priest upon his throne.' Christ foreshadowed. He alone is both King and Priest, uniting the two offices.

'Behold the man whose name is The BRANCH... he shall build the temple of the LORD,' bearing the glory, 'a priest upon his throne.' Christ foreshadowed. He alone is both King and Priest, uniting the two offices.
The crowns shall be a memorial, and 'they that are far off shall come and build' if they obey. A gathered people. The distant shall help build God's house when they heed His voice.
